description: Rust port of psych::fa and FactoMineR::FAMD: exploratory factor analysis on numeric data, factor analysis of mixed data, and a standalone correlation-matrix builder covering the full psych::fa(cor=...) method surface.
release_notes:
Input flexibility: SPSS `.sav` support and inline meta with regex patterns.

- **SPSS `.sav` / `.zsav` input.** `fa` and `famd` now dispatch on the input extension: `.csv`/`.tsv` use the existing CSV reader, `.sav`/`.zsav` go through the bundled `spss-io` library (readstat + polars). SPSS user-defined missing values are coerced to NA on read. When meta leaves fields blank, SPSS variable labels fill in `label` and SPSS value-label presence infers `type` (numeric-with-labels â†’ ordinal, plain numeric â†’ numeric, string â†’ categorical).
- **Inline meta.** `meta` may now be an inline TOML array instead of a path to a CSV. Two entry shapes: row entries (`variable = "..."`) and regex pattern entries (`match = "..."`). Type/batch/label defaults cascade down from pattern entries to all matching columns. Empty-list / zero-match inline metas error up front.
- **Regex variable selection.** Pattern entries use the Rust `regex` crate (no backrefs/lookaround). All matches apply: a column hit by multiple entries is added once per entry, so one column can belong to multiple batches. Unmatched columns are silently dropped.
- **Label templates.** Pattern entries accept a `label_template` with placeholders: `${name}` (full column name), `${0}` (whole regex match), `${1}`, `${2}`, â€¦ (positional captures). Omitting `label_template` falls back to the SPSS variable label (for `.sav`) or the column name (for CSV).
- Existing path-based meta CSVs continue to work without change; the workflow is purely additive.
